tossups.question: Sitting atop a roof, guitarist Julian Bream played a piece by this composer whose theme in 3/4 is [read slowly] eighth-rest E D-sharp E A G-sharp E. Due to this virtuoso’s age-40 death from tuberculosis, he could not realize his obese admirer’s vision of touring Europe with the complete four-book magnum opus that this interpreter’s pianism inspired. Like Asturias (Leyenda), the A minor second piece of this composer’s Impresiones de España is best known in Tárrega’s guitar transcription. For 10 points, what Wikipedia-article-lacking Catalan pianist composed a breezy Serenata española and premiered most movements of Albéniz’s Ibéria?
tossups.question_sanitized: Sitting atop a roof, guitarist Julian Bream played a piece by this composer whose theme in 3/4 is [read slowly] eighth-rest E D-sharp E A G-sharp E. Due to this virtuoso's age-40 death from tuberculosis, he could not realize his obese admirer's vision of touring Europe with the complete four-book magnum opus that this interpreter's pianism inspired. Like Asturias (Leyenda), the A minor second piece of this composer's Impresiones de Espana is best known in Tarrega's guitar transcription. For 10 points, what Wikipedia-article-lacking Catalan pianist composed a breezy Serenata espanola and premiered most movements of Albeniz's Iberia?

tossups.question: In 1990, Matanya Ophee published this golden-haired composer’s ambitious 10 etudes arranged in key sequence. Before absconding, this early Romantic prodigy’s obsessive Italian foster father locked him at home to practice for five hours a day. After moving to London, he promoted a new instrument invented by Wheatstone, the English concertina, with a technique that even uses the pinky. Fernando Sor praised this Genevan composer of a very difficult Introduction et Caprice whose use of tremolo in his Op. 19 Reverie dwarfs and predates Tárrega’s standard Recuerdos. For 10 points, what mid-19th-century classical guitar virtuoso lived slightly later than Coste, Giuliani, and Mertz, and is alphabetized under R?
tossups.question_sanitized: In 1990, Matanya Ophee published this golden-haired composer's ambitious 10 etudes arranged in key sequence. Before absconding, this early Romantic prodigy's obsessive Italian foster father locked him at home to practice for five hours a day. After moving to London, he promoted a new instrument invented by Wheatstone, the English concertina, with a technique that even uses the pinky. Fernando Sor praised this Genevan composer of a very difficult Introduction et Caprice whose use of tremolo in his Op. 19 Reverie dwarfs and predates Tarrega's standard Recuerdos. For 10 points, what mid-19th-century classical guitar virtuoso lived slightly later than Coste, Giuliani, and Mertz, and is alphabetized under R?

tossups.question: Players of this instrument may practice exercises by Mauro Giuliani and Matteo Carcassi and use p-i-m-a notation. This instrument plays both arpeggios and a tremolo melody in a solo piece titled for “Memories of” a certain palace. The Nokia ringtone was excerpted from a piece for this instrument called Gran Vals written by Francisco Tárrega. This instrument is the soloist in (*) Joaquín Rodrigo’s Concierto de Aranjuez (“con-see-AIR-toh day ar-AHN-hwayz”), which was often performed by Andrés Segovia. Players of this instrument may learn how to read tab notation and chord charts. For 10 points, name this six-stringed instrument often strummed in flamenco music.

Players of this instrument may learn how to read tab notation and chord charts. For 10 points, name this six-stringed instrument often strummed in flamenco music.
tossups.answer: guitar [accept specific examples such as classical guitar, acoustic guitar, or flamenco guitar] (The second line is Recuerdos de la Alhambra.)

tossups.question: The musician Reginal Smith Brindle composed settings of the poems of Garcia Lorca for this instrument. J.S. Bach composed an E minor piece titled Bourrée for this instrument. It’s not a violin, but Lee Krasner owned the extremely-rare Rawlins one of these instruments produced by Antonio Stradivari. A strict teacher of this instrument criticised the unorthodox style that John Williams used when playing this instrument with the group Sky. The iconic (*) Nokia theme was taken from Gran Vals, a composition for this instrument by Francisco Tárrega. Joaquín (“wa-KEEN”) Rodrigo composed the Concierto de Aranjuez (“con-see-AIR-to day a-RAHN-ways”) for this instrument. Andrés Segovia and Julian Bream were virtuosos of this instrument. For 10 points, name this instrument which typically has six strings and is related to the lute.
tossups.question_sanitized: The musician Reginal Smith Brindle composed settings of the poems of Garcia Lorca for this instrument. J.S. Bach composed an E minor piece titled Bourree for this instrument. It's not a violin, but Lee Krasner owned the extremely-rare Rawlins one of these instruments produced by Antonio Stradivari. A strict teacher of this instrument criticised the unorthodox style that John Williams used when playing this instrument with the group Sky. The iconic (*) Nokia theme was taken from Gran Vals, a composition for this instrument by Francisco Tarrega. Joaquin ("wa-KEEN") Rodrigo composed the Concierto de Aranjuez ("con-see-AIR-to day a-RAHN-ways") for this instrument. Andres Segovia and Julian Bream were virtuosos of this instrument. For 10 points, name this instrument which typically has six strings and is related to the lute.

tossups.question: A piece for two of these instruments dedicated to Alexandre Lagoya and Ida Presti was inspired by The Well-Tempered Clavier and was composed by Mario Castelnuovo-Tedesco. The 1973 album Together features Julian Bream and the Australian musician John Williams playing this instrument. In the Adagio movement of a concerto for this instrument, it plays B minor chords to introduce an English horn solo. Francisco Tárrega included a difficult tremolo passage for this instrument in his Recuerdos de la Alhambra. Joaquín Rodrigo's Concierto de Aranjuez features one of these instruments. For 10 points, name this six-string instrument played by Andrés Segovia that features prominently in flamenco music from Spain.
tossups.question_sanitized: A piece for two of these instruments dedicated to Alexandre Lagoya and Ida Presti was inspired by The Well-Tempered Clavier and was composed by Mario Castelnuovo-Tedesco. The 1973 album Together features Julian Bream and the Australian musician John Williams playing this instrument. In the Adagio movement of a concerto for this instrument, it plays B minor chords to introduce an English horn solo. Francisco Tarrega included a difficult tremolo passage for this instrument in his Recuerdos de la Alhambra. Joaquin Rodrigo's Concierto de Aranjuez features one of these instruments. For 10 points, name this six-string instrument played by Andres Segovia that features prominently in flamenco music from Spain.

tossups.question: This is the interval between the lower voice and the escape tone in a Landini cadence. It rings out in the long chord held at the beginning of Tárrega’s Capricho árabe (“AH-rah-beh”) for guitar. The woodwinds play a descending scale over this interval to open the final movement of Schubert’s Tragic Symphony. The right hand plays this interval twice at the start of Beethoven’s sonata The Hunt, and the solo horn plays it at the beginning of Bruckner’s 4th Symphony. The cellos and basses play this interval to begin their unison solo in the last movement of (*) Beethoven’s 9th Symphony. This interval corresponds to a 3:2 frequency ratio and is equivalent to seven semitones. A key’s dominant is exactly this interval above the tonic. For 10 points, the first two different notes played in “Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star” are separated by what interval, which separates the notes C and G?
tossups.question_sanitized: This is the interval between the lower voice and the escape tone in a Landini cadence. It rings out in the long chord held at the beginning of Tarrega's Capricho arabe ("AH-rah-beh") for guitar. The woodwinds play a descending scale over this interval to open the final movement of Schubert's Tragic Symphony. The right hand plays this interval twice at the start of Beethoven's sonata The Hunt, and the solo horn plays it at the beginning of Bruckner's 4th Symphony. The cellos and basses play this interval to begin their unison solo in the last movement of (*) Beethoven's 9th Symphony. This interval corresponds to a 3:2 frequency ratio and is equivalent to seven semitones. A key's dominant is exactly this interval above the tonic. For 10 points, the first two different notes played in "Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star" are separated by what interval, which separates the notes C and G?

tossups.question: "Lágrima," "Endecha," and "Oremus" are the three named pieces in this genre by Francisco Tarrega, who wrote 14 standard examples of them for guitar. Pieces subtitled "Homage to Bach" and "Lyrical Melody" are among the set of five pieces in this genre for guitar by Heitor Villa-Lobos ("ay-TOR VEE-lah LOH-boos"). In a piece in this genre for piano, the left hand plays a repeated A-flat, possibly intended to evoke a (*) drizzle the composer experienced on Majorca ("mah-YOR-kah"). Sergei Rachmaninoff's pieces in this genre span all 24 major and minor keys, as does the Opus 28 set of them by Frederic Chopin. Pieces in this genre are paired with fugues in The Well-Tempered Clavier. For 10 points, give this musical genre whose name indicates that it is meant to precede another piece.
tossups.question_sanitized: "Lagrima," "Endecha," and "Oremus" are the three named pieces in this genre by Francisco Tarrega, who wrote 14 standard examples of them for guitar. Pieces subtitled "Homage to Bach" and "Lyrical Melody" are among the set of five pieces in this genre for guitar by Heitor Villa-Lobos ("ay-TOR VEE-lah LOH-boos"). In a piece in this genre for piano, the left hand plays a repeated A-flat, possibly intended to evoke a (*) drizzle the composer experienced on Majorca ("mah-YOR-kah"). Sergei Rachmaninoff's pieces in this genre span all 24 major and minor keys, as does the Opus 28 set of them by Frederic Chopin. Pieces in this genre are paired with fugues in The Well-Tempered Clavier. For 10 points, give this musical genre whose name indicates that it is meant to precede another piece.

tossups.question: Note to moderator: Read the description of the melody in the pre-For 10 points clue very slowly.A one-minute long Marc-André Hamelin piece based on this melody ends with seven pairs of notes played mezzo staccato. This is definitely the most recognizable melody ever employed by the composer of “Recuerdos de la Alhambra.” It originally comes from the Gran Vals by Francisco Tárrega. This melody consists of: eighth notes on D and E followed by quarter notes on lower F and G, then the same (*) four notes shifted down two, then the same four notes shifted down one more, then a dotted half note on a higher A. For 10 points, name this tune which might be the world’s most commonly heard melody because it’s used as a ringtone by a Finnish telecom company.
tossups.answer: the Nokia tune [accept Grande Valse before Gran Vals is read]

tossups.question: Fritz Kreisler convinced his own concert manager to arrange this musician’s wildly successful debut 1928 American tour. This musician frequently performed a transcription of the Christmas song “El Noi de la Mare” (el NOY deh lah MAH-ruh) as an encore piece. Heitor Villa-Lobos (ai-TOR vee-yuh-LOW-bowsh) transformed street music from this man’s country into a set of twelve etudes dedicated to this musician, who also commissioned the Fantasia for a Gentleman. This musician and his teacher, Miguel Llobet (yoh-BET), worked together to make Isaac Albeniz’s music a key part of their instrument’s repertoire. Breaking from tradition established by Fernando Sor and Francisco Tarrega, this musician played using a combination of both fingernails and fingertips, and he endorsed the use of nylon rather than catgut strings. For 10 points, name this Spanish musician, the father of classical guitar.
tossups.answer: Andrés Segovia [or Andrés Segovia Torres, 1st Marquis of Salobreña]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Andres Segovia [or Andres Segovia Torres, 1st Marquis of Salobrena]

For 10 points, name this Spanish musician, the father of classical guitar.

tossups.question: This composer included “Il tuo squardo,” “Moriro!!,” and “Tho riveduta in sogno” in his set of six Italian ballads. A suite by this composer includes a piece that begins with a “tri-po-let one [pause] and-uh” rhythm in E-flat major and is titled Sous le Palmier. The opening of a piano prelude by this composer instructs the right thumb to play a melody mimicking rasgueado chords while the index finger plays an open-string-like pedal point. He collaborated on three operas with his patron Francis Money-Coutts. An F-minor impression of a polo match is the eighth movement in his most famous composition. Many of his works, including Leyenda, were transcribed for guitar by Francisco Tárrega. He wrote a twelve-part suite whose A-flat minor opening movement incorporates the jota and the Fandango and is titled Evocación.For 10 points, name this Romantic composer who depicted several Andalusian cities in his immensely difficulty Iberia.
tossups.answer: Isaac Albéniz [or Isaac Manuel Francisco Albéniz y Pascual]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Isaac Albeniz [or Isaac Manuel Francisco Albeniz y Pascual]

tossups.question: Roland Dyens composed an Austin Tango for four of these instruments. One piece written for this instrument contains the movements Preludio Saudade, Andante Religioso, and Allegro. Oscar Ghiglia plays this instrument, as do Manuel Barrueco and Julian Bream. One composer for this instrument composed the pieces Elogio de la Danza and El Decameron Negro. That composer is Leo (*) Brouwer. Another piece written for this instrument develops a haunting melody with a tremolo technique in which the a, m, and i fingers play the same note in quick succession. That piece is Recuerdos de Alhambra, written by Francisco Tarrega. For 10 points, name this instrument for which "Silent Night" was originally composed and which is played by Andres Segovia.

That piece is Recuerdos de Alhambra, written by Francisco Tarrega. For 10 points, name this instrument for which "Silent Night" was originally composed and which is played by Andres Segovia.

tossups.question: Hans Werner Henze composed two sonatas “on Shakespearean characters” for this instrument, and published them under the title Royal Winter Music. Many students of this instrument practice by learning the etudes composed by Matteo Carcassi. Two of these instruments play the Fuga elegiaca composed by Mario Castelnuovo-Tedesco, who specialized in writing music for it. In the Adagio second movement of a concerto for this instrument, it plays broken B-minor chords before an English horn introduces the main melody. Benjamin Britten composed Noctural for a virtuoso of this instrument, Julian Bream. Francisco Tarrega composed many works for this instrument, which is accompanied by the orchestra in Joaquin Rodrigo’s Concierto de Aranjuez. For 10 points, name this instrument played by Andres Segovia, which is historically prominent in Spain and has six strings.

Both Joaquin Rodrigo, the blind composer of the Concierto de Aranjuez, and Francisco Tárrega hailed from this country, where the flamenco tradition makes prominent use of the guitar.

Though this Spanish composer never actually composed for the guitar, many transcriptions of his works have become part of the guitar repertoire, such as his Asturias. His magnum opus is the difficult piano suite Iberia.

bonuses.leadin: The melody of a piece for this instrument by Francisco Tárrega, the Grand Vals, was used in a popular Nokia ringtone. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this string instrument of Spanish origin. Its music often accompanies flamenco dancing along with palmas, or hand clapping.

Unlike his Mandolin Concerto in C major, this Baroque composer’s Lute Concerto in D major is usually played today on the guitar. His short Double Trumpet Concerto in C major, RV 537, is one of the earliest solo brass pieces.

This master Spanish guitarist, who refused to play with his ring finger or fingernails, composed Variations on a Theme of Mozart. His book Méthode pour la Guitare (“may-TUD poor la ghee-TAR”) was one of the most popular pedagogical books of the 19th century.
answers: Fernando Sor [or Ferran Sor; or Fernando Sors or Fernando Sort or Fernando Sorts]

bonuses.leadin_sanitized: According to the composer's wife Victoria Kamhi, the adagio of a concerto for this instrument was inspired by a miscarriage, and not the bombing of Guernica. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this string instrument featured in Joaquin ("wah-KEEN") Rodrigo's Concierto de Aranjuez ("ah-rahn-WEZZ"). Mario Castelnuovo-Tedesco and Francisco Tarrega primarily wrote for this instrument, an electric version of which was created by Les Paul ("less Paul").
A modified guitar with thinner tops is used for this genre of music, whose forms are called palos. Dancers to this music sometimes use castanets.

This composer wrote a set of twelve etudes for the guitar virtuoso Andres Segovia and five guitar preludes for his wife Arminda. The first of this composer's Choros ("SHOW-roos") is scored for guitar.

bonuses.leadin: Among this man’s lesser known works are the operas Merlin and Henry Clifford. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Catalan composer of the piano suite Iberia.
Albeniz is from this nation, whose regions he depicted in his Suite Española.
Albeniz’s work Asturias (Leyenda) was transcribed for this instrument by Francisco Tarrega. Famous players of it include Julian Bream and Andres Segovia.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Among this man's lesser known works are the operas Merlin and Henry Clifford. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this Catalan composer of the piano suite Iberia.
Albeniz is from this nation, whose regions he depicted in his Suite Espanola.
Albeniz's work Asturias (Leyenda) was transcribed for this instrument by Francisco Tarrega. Famous players of it include Julian Bream and Andres Segovia.

bonuses.leadin: In this piece, inspired by the composer’s visit to Granada, the “i,” “m,” and “a” fingers play a melody entirely tremolo as the thumb plays a counter-melody. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this virtuoso guitar piece by Francisco Tarrega.

This guitarist and composer of fifteen guitar quartets gave a guitar to his friend Hector Berlioz. He commissioned Harold in Italy from Berlioz after acquiring a Stradivarius viola, but was dissatisfied with the solo part.

This contemporary Australian classical guitarist commissioned guitar concertos from Andre Previn and Stephen Dodgson. He shares his name with a composer of film scores known for collaborating with Steven Spielberg and George Lucas.
answers: Recuerdos de la Alhambra [or Memories of the Alhambra]
answers_sanitized: Recuerdos de la Alhambra [or Memories of the Alhambra]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In this piece, inspired by the composer's visit to Granada, the "i," "m," and "a" fingers play a melody entirely tremolo as the thumb plays a counter-melody. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this virtuoso guitar piece by Francisco Tarrega.

This guitarist and composer of fifteen guitar quartets gave a guitar to his friend Hector Berlioz. He commissioned Harold in Italy from Berlioz after acquiring a Stradivarius viola, but was dissatisfied with the solo part.

This contemporary Australian classical guitarist commissioned guitar concertos from Andre Previn and Stephen Dodgson. He shares his name with a composer of film scores known for collaborating with Steven Spielberg and George Lucas.

bonuses.leadin: Clara Schumann convinced Brahms to recast a piece for two pianos into one for this ensemble in F minor, which opens with a unison theme that accentuates the half-step from D-flat to C. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this ensemble whose chief example Robert Schumann wrote in his “Chamber Music Year” 1842 for keyboard plus string quartet. Schubert’s work for this ensemble named “Trout” quotes his song “Die Forelle.”

Saint-Saëns stormed off the stage after premiering this Belgian composer’s sensual piano quintet as they both liked Irish composer Augusta Holmès. He used cyclic form in his organ music, violin sonata for Ysaÿe (“ee-ZAI”), and three-movement Symphony in D minor.

A slow fugue begins the Franck-inspired Opus 1 piano quintet of this Spanish composer from Seville. His pact with Albéniz and Falla to reject French idioms led to the Danzas fantásticas, Homenaje a Tárrega for guitar, and Fandanguillo.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Clara Schumann convinced Brahms to recast a piece for two pianos into one for this ensemble in F minor, which opens with a unison theme that accentuates the half-step from D-flat to C. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this ensemble whose chief example Robert Schumann wrote in his "Chamber Music Year" 1842 for keyboard plus string quartet. Schubert's work for this ensemble named "Trout" quotes his song "Die Forelle."

Saint-Saens stormed off the stage after premiering this Belgian composer's sensual piano quintet as they both liked Irish composer Augusta Holmes. He used cyclic form in his organ music, violin sonata for Ysaye ("ee-ZAI"), and three-movement Symphony in D minor.

A slow fugue begins the Franck-inspired Opus 1 piano quintet of this Spanish composer from Seville. His pact with Albeniz and Falla to reject French idioms led to the Danzas fantasticas, Homenaje a Tarrega for guitar, and Fandanguillo.

bonuses.leadin: This composer's most ambitious choral piece was used as a model for a 1947 piece by Maurice Duruflé in the same genre. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this composer who set nine poems by Paul Verlaine (vair-LEN) in the song cycle La bonne chanson.
Fauré's Dolly Suite ends with a lively dance in the style of this country's music. Emmanuel Chabrier (shob-ree-AY) and Maurice Ravel both wrote rhapsodies titled for this country.
Spanish composers Joaquín Rodrigo and Francisco Tárrega wrote concertos and solo pieces for this instrument, whose six strings may be plucked either with one's fingers or with a pick.
answers_sanitized: Spain [or "Le pas espagnol"; or Espana; or Rapsodie espagnole]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This composer's most ambitious choral piece was used as a model for a 1947 piece by Maurice Durufle in the same genre. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this composer who set nine poems by Paul Verlaine (vair-LEN) in the song cycle La bonne chanson.
Faure's Dolly Suite ends with a lively dance in the style of this country's music. Emmanuel Chabrier (shob-ree-AY) and Maurice Ravel both wrote rhapsodies titled for this country.
Spanish composers Joaquin Rodrigo and Francisco Tarrega wrote concertos and solo pieces for this instrument, whose six strings may be plucked either with one's fingers or with a pick.

bonuses.leadin: Francisco Tárrega’s challenging “tremolo étude” for guitar is titled Recuerdos de, or Memories of, this location. For 10 points each:
parts: Both Joaquín Rodrigo’s Junto al Generalife and the first movement of de Falla’s Nights in the Gardens of Spain were inspired by a palace adjacent to what large Moorish castle in Granada?

de Falla’s postcard from the Alhambra might have inspired this composer’s piano habaneras, like the duet Lindaraja, a prelude titled “La puerta del vino” at the end of the score, and “Evening in Granada” from Estampes.

This composer of El ultimo trémolo wrote two movements inspired by an organist playing Bach on a visit to Montevideo’s Basílica de San José. This Paraguayan guitarist later added a “Preludio Saudade” to his La Catedral.
answers_sanitized: Agustin Barrios Mangore [accept either underlined portion]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Francisco Tarrega's challenging "tremolo etude" for guitar is titled Recuerdos de, or Memories of, this location. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Both Joaquin Rodrigo's Junto al Generalife and the first movement of de Falla's Nights in the Gardens of Spain were inspired by a palace adjacent to what large Moorish castle in Granada?

de Falla's postcard from the Alhambra might have inspired this composer's piano habaneras, like the duet Lindaraja, a prelude titled "La puerta del vino" at the end of the score, and "Evening in Granada" from Estampes.

This composer of El ultimo tremolo wrote two movements inspired by an organist playing Bach on a visit to Montevideo's Basilica de San Jose. This Paraguayan guitarist later added a "Preludio Saudade" to his La Catedral.

bonuses.leadin: One composer from this country wrote Love, the Magician and music for the ballet The Three-Cornered Hat. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this country, also the homeland of Isaac Albeniz. An opera singer from this country was one of the "Three Tenors" along with Pavarotti and Carreras.

Spanish composer Francisco Tarrega transcribed many pieces by classical composers like Bach and Mozart for this instrument. It was played by Andres Segovia, and its electric type was developed by Les Paul.
This Spain-inspired piece by Rimsky-Korsakov contains five movements, including a set of variations on a theme played by the horns in between two similar Alboradas.
answers_sanitized: Capriccio Espagnol [or Capriccio on Spanish Themes]

Spanish composer Francisco Tarrega transcribed many pieces by classical composers like Bach and Mozart for this instrument. It was played by Andres Segovia, and its electric type was developed by Les Paul.
This Spain-inspired piece by Rimsky-Korsakov contains five movements, including a set of variations on a theme played by the horns in between two similar Alboradas.
